posts.text:
"Roll the dice enough times, and the odds eventually work against you," RAND's Raphael Cohen says of Tehran's "escalate to de-escalate" strategy.

"Iran is about to learn this lesson, and it will have only itself to blame." 
bit.ly/4rCCgBN

posts.text:
A prolonged Israeli operation in Lebanon could undermine Lebanon's institutions, turn its people against Israel, and further entrench Hezbollah's narrative, says RAND's Shira Efron.

"That's precisely the opposite of what Israel and the region need." nytimes.com/2026/03/19/opini…

posts.text: The term "rural" is a kaleidoscope of conflicting definitions, says RAND's David Luckey. This lack of clarity "actively distorts resource allocation and could put the lives of rural Americans at risk." bit.ly/4uaeVdr
posts.quote: The term "rural" is a kaleidoscope of conflicting definitions, says RAND's David Luckey. This lack of clarity "actively distorts resource allocation and could put the lives of rural Americans at risk." bit.ly/4uaeVdr

posts.text: RAND experts propose a framework for disarmament, demobilization, and reintegration in Gaza, which would create opportunities and incentives for Hamas fighters to disarm and transition to civilian life. bit.ly/3ZI7qMP

posts.text:
China is facing a population upheaval.

How China handles the crisis will shape not only its own future but also the stability of the region and the global economy. bit.ly/40uybVi

posts.text:
"The current war against Iran could be a watershed moment for Israel's security," says Raphael Cohen.

"The geopolitical landscape of the Middle East—at least insofar as which countries are on Israel's side—may look very different once the dust settles." bit.ly/4sXkGtB
